title: Setting milestone dates based on the launch date
excerpt: Hi, I am trying to find a solution to one of the biggest problems I have with using monday. I have many projects that run multiple times a year, same milestones, same owner. I currently have an excel file that uses formu…

posts.author: jennistrong34 (Jennifer Gallant)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, I am trying to find a solution to one of the biggest problems I have with using monday. I have many projects that run multiple times a year, same milestones, same owner. I currently have an excel file that uses formulas that automatically populates all the dates in my schedule when I put in the launch date. This formula is based on weeks out from the launch and a template that I used when I used Wrike. So I can easily populate my dates in my excel file each time the program runs and then I have to manually add those to the same schedule in monday. Is there a way to set one date in a monday schedule that will autopopulate the remaining dates based on weeks out?

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @jennistrong34

Our partner Work Perfect, contact @Kimb, is launching an app that works like Wrike. You can have a complete schedule in a template and just change the starting date (anchor), all dependencies will populate the correct dates (skipping weekends) for all items in the dependency chain.

posts.author: jennistrong34 (Jennifer Gallant)
posts.cookedHtml: That would be amazing! Thank you so much. Is this app launched already?

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml: @kimb has all the details

posts.author: Kimb (Kim Berney)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @jennistrong34 please email me at kim@workperfect.io. We are launching the app very soon : )

posts.author: Work_Perfect_Apps (Work Perfect Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @mray, yes !
You can find it in the app marketplace under the name ‘Scheduler Pro’
